{{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of burial mounds from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - Kanan Town Homepage {{Pref-stub|pref=Osaka}} [[Category:Parks in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:1986 establishments in Japan]] [[ja:大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%98%AA%E5%BA%9C%E7%AB%8B%E8%BF%91%E3%81%A4%E9%A3%9B%E9%B3%A5%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

{{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of burial mounds from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka

(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of burial mounds from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of burial mounds from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of burial mounds from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of burial mounds from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - Kanan Town Homepage {{Pref-stub|pref=Osaka}} [[Category:Parks in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of burial mounds from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round burial mounds, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of Kofuns from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round Kofuns, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of Kofuns from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * 102 round Kofuns, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). * 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of Kofuns from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} ** 102 round Kofuns, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). ** 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of Kofuns from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} ** 102 round Kofuns, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). ** 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. {{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of Kofuns from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} ** 102 round Kofuns, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). ** 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - Kanan Town Homepage {{Pref-stub|pref=Osaka}} [[Category:Parks in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:1986 establishments in Japan]] [[ja:大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%98%AA%E5%BA%9C%E7%AB%8B%E8%BF%91%E3%81%A4%E9%A3%9B%E9%B3%A5%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox park | name = Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka | native_name = 大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 | english_name = Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka
Historical Park | image = [[File:近つ飛鳥風土記の丘 緑の広場 2013.3.30 - panoramio.jpg|300px]]　 | image_caption = {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|coord={{coord|34|30|11.2|N|135|38|37.6|E}}}} | country = {{JPN}} | location = {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} | latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 30 |latitude_sec = 11.2 | longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 38 |longitude_sec = 37.6 | type = [[Historic site]] park ({{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}}) | area = 29 ha | opened = [[1986]] (Shōwa 61), June | operator = Osaka Prefecture | status = Open year-round | parking = Available, 30 spaces (plus 80 spaces at the museum) | office = {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}
(separate management building within the park) | office_address = 299 Higashiyama, Kanan, Minamikawachi District, Osaka Prefecture | phone = 0721-93-8321 | website = [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka (Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Official Website)] }} '''Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka''' (大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘) is a [[historic park]] located in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|ja|南河内郡}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/annai/shisetsu/detail.php?recid=39　Public Facility Guide - Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum (Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka)] Osaka Prefectural Government Website. The park preserves the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, a nationally designated [[historic site]]. == Overview == During the 1965–1974 period (Shōwa 40s), the {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}}, located in the hills of {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} and surrounding areas, was at risk of destruction due to the development of the {{ill|Yamato Danchi|ja|大和団地}} [[New Towns in Japan|new town]] project and the construction of the {{ill|Hannan Neopolis|ja|阪南ネオポリス}}. In response, Osaka Prefecture purchased a 29-hectare section containing the densest concentration of Kofuns from the development company to preserve them. The area was developed with pathways integrated into the kofun cluster and opened to the public in June [[1986]] (Shōwa 61). On [[October 7, 1994]] (Heisei 6), the park was designated a [[historic site]] by the national government. This facility is a historic site park intended for cultural heritage preservation and public education, rather than a [[urban park]][https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/material/files/group/15/R3toshikeikakumasterplan.pdf　Kanan Town Urban Planning Master Plan (March 2021)] p.23. == Facilities == * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} ** 102 round Kofuns, approximately 15 meters in diameter (some square-shaped). ** 40 of the tombs have {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chambers) accessible for public viewing. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} – Exhibits artifacts excavated from the kofun cluster. Opened in 1994 (Heisei 6). * Two observation decks – Offering views of the "Chikatsu Asuka" region, the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, designated as a [[World Heritage Site]], as well as distant views of [[Osaka City]], the Rokko mountain range, and {{ill|Awaji Island|ja|淡路島}}[https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] Kanan Town Homepage, accessed October 2, 2022. * Green Plaza * Cherry Blossom Trees * Plum Grove – Approximately 140 plum trees behind the museum. * Rest Areas * Two Public Toilets * Free Parking (30 spaces) [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/static/pdf/fudoki/fuudoki_map.pdf　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka Map] == Visitor Information == * Opening time: 09:30 * Closing time: 17:00 == Transportation Access == * From [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]], take the {{ill|Kongō Bus|ja|金剛自動車}} Ishikawa Line (from [[Tondabayashi Station]]) or Hannan Line (from [[Kishi Station]]), and alight at "Chikatsu Asuka Museum-mae" bus stop. * About 10 minutes by car from [[Minami-Hanna Road]] {{ill|Habikino-Higashi Interchange|ja|羽曳野東インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Taishi Interchange|ja|太子インターチェンジ}}. [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/jouhou/tikatsu.html　Overview of Chikatsu Asuka Museum (including detailed transportation information)] Osaka Prefecture Website. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *{{Cite book|ja|author=Zoku Kanan Town History Compilation Committee|title=Zoku Kanan Town History|publisher={{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}|year=2004|date=September 2004|ref=harv}} *{{Cite journal|ja|author=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Bulletin|issue=10|publisher={{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}|year=2006|date=2006|ref=harv}}== See Also == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|ja|一須賀古墳群}} * {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}} * {{ill|Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/?s=fudoki　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Homepage * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/search/detail/sightseeing_273.html Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] – OSAKA INFO (Osaka Tourism Information) * [https://www.town.kanan.osaka.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/rekishi_bunka/2/3037.html　Chikatsu Asuka Fudoki-No-Oka] - Kanan Town Homepage {{Pref-stub|pref=Osaka}} [[Category:Parks in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:1986 establishments in Japan]] [[ja:大阪府立近つ飛鳥風土記の丘]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%98%AA%E5%BA%9C%E7%AB%8B%E8%BF%91%E3%81%A4%E9%A3%9B%E9%B3%A5%E9%A2%A8%E5%9C%9F%E8%A8%98%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%98]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Fudoki Hill]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}
